title: Book Of Nonsense Limerick 2.
content:
There was a Young Lady of Ryde,
    Whose shoe-strings were seldom untied;
    She purchased some clogs,
    And some small spotty dogs,
    And frequently walked about Ryde.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Lear, titled "Book Of Nonsense Limerick 2.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
